Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Dustin M. Seesemann, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Edward Magee.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendants MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY and NATIONAL LUMBER AND SUPPLY, INC.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendants MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY and NATIONAL LUMBER AND SUPPLY, INC., in the amount of $440,796.49 in Economic Damages, $1,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $1,440,796.49.
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE

No appearance by plaintiff, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion to continue the trial date is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant made an affirmative showing of good cause to continue the trial date, as required by CRC 3.1332. The trial date, which has a current return date of September 29, 2014, is continued to December 8, 2014 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Discovery remains closed. Order signed in open court. MINI MINUTES:

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF PATHOLOGY

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion to compel production of pathology is denied without prejudice. The Court does not have authority to consider this motion. (See C.C.P. 2024.020; Pelton-Shepherd Industries v. Delta Packaging Products (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1568.) Order signed in open court.
